categories.name: Exposed Control Panel
categories.description: AI agent control panel accessible from the public internet without authentication. Includes admin interfaces, configuration dashboards, and management consoles for OpenClaw/MoltBot/ClawdBot instances that are reachable without any form of authentication.
categories.detection_method: HTTP fingerprinting of known control panel response patterns, default page content matching, admin endpoint enumeration.
categories.remediation: Restrict control panel access to authenticated users. Bind to localhost or private network interface. Place behind VPN or zero-trust access proxy.

categories.name: RCE-Vulnerable Instance
categories.description: AI agent instance with known remote code execution vulnerabilities. Includes instances running outdated platform versions with published CVEs that allow arbitrary command execution through the agent's tool-use interface or platform API.
categories.detection_method: Version fingerprinting cross-referenced against CVE databases. CISA KEV matching. Exploit signature detection on exposed endpoints.
categories.remediation: Patch to latest version. Restrict tool-use permissions to minimum required scope. Implement input validation on all agent tool interfaces.

categories.name: Malicious ClawHub Skill
categories.description: AI agent skill distributed through the ClawHub marketplace containing C2 callbacks, backdoors, credential harvesting, or data exfiltration logic. Five confirmed malicious skills identified with mapped C2 infrastructure.
categories.detection_method: Static analysis of skill source for outbound HTTP calls to non-allowlisted domains. Obfuscated code detection. Behavioral monitoring of skill execution for unexpected filesystem, network, or credential access patterns.
categories.remediation: Remove malicious skills. Audit all installed skills against known-malicious hashes (published via DECLAWED TAXII feed). Implement skill allowlisting. Verify skill publisher identity.

categories.name: APT-Linked Infrastructure
categories.description: Exposed AI agent instance hosted on infrastructure with known APT (Advanced Persistent Threat) activity associations. Includes instances on IPs, ASNs, or hosting providers previously used by tracked threat actors.
categories.detection_method: Cross-reference instance hosting infrastructure against threat intelligence feeds for known APT-associated IPs, ASNs, and hosting providers.
categories.remediation: Migrate AI agent workloads to trusted infrastructure. Implement network segmentation between AI agents and other workloads. Monitor for lateral movement indicators.

categories.name: Breach-Linked Instance
categories.description: Exposed AI agent instance on infrastructure previously associated with data breach activity. Includes instances hosted on IPs or networks that have appeared in breach notification databases or incident response reports.
categories.detection_method: Cross-reference instance IPs against breach databases, incident response reports, and data leak monitoring services.
categories.remediation: Audit infrastructure for compromise indicators. Bind AI agent services to specific interfaces rather than 0.0.0.0. Implement network-level access controls.
